title: NEO_APP_PROFILE (machine-authoritative app profile, NEO 3.0)
description: PROPOSED machine-authoritative JSON schema for the per-app NEO profile. In 3.0 this JSON is the SOLE authority; the legacy Markdown profile (modules/<app>/NEO_APP_PROFILE.md) becomes a generated, non-authoritative VIEW (see PROFILE_MARKDOWN_VIEW_DESIGN.md). This schema consolidates BOTH legacy sources the matrix homes to JSON_PROFILE: the NEO_APP_PROFILE.md KEY:value directives (drive AS1/AS3/AS4/AS5/AS6/AS7/AS9/AS10/AS11/AS14/AS15/AS17 + E5) AND the NEO_APP.md app-memory template (product purpose, architecture, environments, locked invariants). A profile RECORDS constraints; it does NOT grant edit permission (governance_semantics.records_constraints_not_permission). Runtime-computed binding hashes / pin_records do NOT live here (a file cannot carry its own hash); they live in the host registry and END evidence (see BINDING_FIELDS_DESIGN.md). This file is a Session-4 PROPOSAL under .neo/_v3.0/session4/ and is NOT promoted into live .neo/schema/.

binding.description: AUTHORED binding declarations carried in the profile itself. The COMPUTED binding fields (this profile's own SHA, START/evidence-consumed/live-END hashes, schema SHA, checker SHA, pin_records) are NOT authored here - they are produced by the host registry and the END evidence per BINDING_FIELDS_DESIGN.md. Models the authored half of CHK §4 machine-authority bindings.

profile_schema_id.description: The $id of the schema this instance claims to conform to. The port binds this to the pinned schema SHA in the registry.

profile_schema_version.description: SemVer of the NEO_APP_PROFILE schema this instance targets. Bound to a schema-SHA pin_record in the registry (BINDING_FIELDS_DESIGN B2).

profile_semver.description: SemVer of THIS profile's content. Bumped by every profile-edit session (PROFILE_EDIT_SESSION_CONTROLS.md). Monotonic; the registry records the pre/post values.

neo_governance_version.description: The NEO governance version this profile was authored/ratified under (e.g. 'v3.0'). Part of the §4 NEO-version binding.

generated_view_is_authoritative.description: Invariant guard: the Markdown VIEW is NEVER authority. Always false. The drift lint (PROFILE_MARKDOWN_VIEW_DESIGN.md) fails if a generated view is treated as source.

supersedes_markdown_profile.description: Repo-relative path of the legacy Markdown profile this JSON supersedes as authority (e.g. modules/<app>/NEO_APP_PROFILE.md). Recorded for the port; the JSON is authoritative regardless.

identity.description: Profile identity directives (legacy APP_SLUG / APP_NAME / REAL_APP_PATH). Drives PROF-DIRECTIVES; the canonical home path is fixed by doctrine.

app_slug.description: Lowercase-kebab app slug; the canonical home is modules/<app_slug>/. Used by E-MANDATORY to decide the app-evidence checker must run.

real_app_path.description: REAL_APP_PATH: the external real-app working tree. RECORDING this path does NOT grant access to it (governance_semantics + boundary_approval gate that).

governance_semantics.description: Models PROF-RECORDS-NOT-GRANT: a profile records constraints and never itself grants edit/real-mode permission; that separation is explicit and machine-checkable.

records_constraints_not_permission.description: Invariant: TRUE. The presence of constraints in this profile never implies permission to act on the app.

real_mode_requires_separate_signoff.description: Invariant: TRUE. Real-app execution requires explicit per-path Raphael boundary sign-off recorded in boundary_approval AND a host-registry approval (AS1 / PROF-BOUNDARY).

secret_values_forbidden.description: Invariant: TRUE. Key names may appear; secret VALUES never (D7). Profile-edit controls and the secret-shaped scan enforce this.

boundary_approval.description: Models PROF-BOUNDARY + PROF-EXPIRY + AS1. The BOUNDARY_APPROVED flag plus the scoped, dated, expiring approval grants and the NOT_APPROVED_FOR constraints. A grant here is mirrored by a host-registry approval_scope_and_expiry record (HOST_REGISTRY 4.3); the registry record is the binding authority for real-mode invocation.

boundary_approved.description: Legacy BOUNDARY_APPROVED (yes/no -> true/false). Real mode requires true AND a current (non-expired) scoped_approval AND the operator switch AND a registry approval (AS1).

not_approved_for.description: NOT_APPROVED_FOR lines, e.g. 'general future real-app execution', 'production apply/deploy without separate explicit approval'. Honored by AS1 / release discipline.

fixture_proof_never_becomes_real_permiss.description: PROF-EXPIRY invariant: TRUE. Fixture proof never silently becomes real-app permission; every grant is session-scoped, path-scoped, dated, and expires at closeout.

scoped_approvals.description: Each explicit Raphael sign-off. Legacy APPROVED_BY/DATE/PATH/SESSION/USE collapse into one entry. PROF-EXPIRY: each is bounded + time-boxed; an expired or non-current grant does not authorize real mode.

approved_by.description: Granting authority (e.g. 'Raphael'). A question-gate is NOT an approval (Session-0 L2).

approved_session.description: Session id the grant is scoped to (APPROVED_SESSION).

path_scope.description: Exact path(s)/surface the grant covers (APPROVED_PATH), including scoped unlocks. Real mode outside these paths is unauthorized.

approved_use.description: Narrative scope of the approved work (APPROVED_USE).

declared_tier.description: Tier the grant was issued at.

status.description: active only while current. Grants expire at their session closeout (PROF-EXPIRY).

expires_at.description: Expiry. May be an ISO date or the token 'session_closeout'. After expiry the grant cannot authorize real mode; a new grant is required.

registry_approval_id.description: Cross-reference to the host-registry approval_scope_and_expiry record (HOST_REGISTRY 4.3). The registry record is the binding authority; this profile field is the human-readable mirror.

approval_history.description: Append-only closeout/baseline ledger (the legacy '## ... closeout' comment lines). RECORDED facts (PR ids, merge commits, current baseline, applied migrations); not themselves a grant. Read by humans and carried across sessions.

migrations_applied.description: Migration ids applied to staging/prod by this session.

denylist.description: Models AS4 (forbidden-path / locked-file denylist) and the locked-file recorded SHAs. Every DENY line from the legacy profile, glob or exact, with optional recorded byte-SHA for re-locked files.

pattern.description: Repo-relative path or glob (entries ending in /** or containing * are globs). No changed file may match (AS4).

is_glob.description: True when the pattern is a glob (e.g. frontend/src/components/dashboard/**).

recorded_sha256.description: Optional post-session byte-SHA of record for a re-locked exact file (e.g. charges.ts). Lets the port detect a silent edit to a locked file, not just a changed-set hit.

note.description: Provenance, e.g. 're-locked 2026-06-11 at rt4-pending-tenant closeout; future change needs a fresh ratified unlock'.

scoped_deny_imports.description: Models AS17 (SCOPED_DENY_IMPORT, script-enforced, never self-attested). A source file that must not import/call a forbidden symbol/module set. The 'barrel blocker' token (e.g. 'index') is just another forbidden symbol entry.

source.description: Repo-relative source file the scope applies to, e.g. backend/src/logic/depositsHeld.ts.

forbidden_symbols.description: Symbols/modules the source may not import via static/dynamic import or require (case/slash/alias-normalized). Include the barrel-blocker token (e.g. 'index').

rationale.description: Why scoped, e.g. 'deposit branch must never reach payment-pool writers / charge pipeline'.

residue_tokens.description: Models AS5: TOKEN markers that must have zero hits across the whole app tree at END (e.g. V26X_, NEO_SCRATCH_, DO_NOT_SHIP).

i18n.description: Models AS6: i18n parity directives + the dynamic-key acceptance escape. Optional when the app has no i18n surface (absent => AS6 NA).

dir.description: I18N_DIR, repo-relative root of the locale dictionaries.

locales.description: I18N_LOCALES. Parity enforced: identical namespace-file sets + identical flattened key paths across all locales; missing/extra keys FAIL.

dynamic_keys_accepted.description: I18N_DYNAMIC_KEYS_ACCEPTED. Dynamic/computed keys FAIL CLOSED unless this is true AND recorded in HUMAN_ACCEPTANCE.md (these are financial pages).

charset.description: Models AS7 (+ D10): files matching these globs must be ASCII-only.

migrations.description: Models AS10: migration directory + the rollback-proof-required rule (PRE-USE vs POST-USE distinction).

rollback_proof_required.description: Any new/edited migration file, or DDL token in a changed file, requires ROLLBACK_PROOF.md plus staging-apply evidence where applicable.

dependencies.description: Models AS9 (DEP-GUARD): the dependency/lockfile files whose change requires a DEP-GUARD evidence pack approved BEFORE install.

risk_tokens.description: Models AS11 (auth) + AS14 (financial): detector token lists. The classifier may only UPGRADE risk from a token hit, never downgrade.

auth_tokens.description: AUTH_TOKEN list (e.g. owner_user_id, req.user, SECURITY DEFINER, ROW LEVEL SECURITY). A hit in a changed file requires explicit HUMAN_ACCEPTANCE.md evidence (AS11).

fin_tokens.description: FIN_TOKEN list (e.g. payment, charge, ledger, balance, payout, amount, cents). Drives money-logic classification (AS14).

commands.description: Models the CMD_* directives (typecheck/build) read for RT2 and recorded as command evidence (AS13). Extensible map of named commands.

command.description: Exact command string. Evidence must record cmd + cwd + exit_code + timestamp + output path.

custom_checks.description: Models AS15 + E5: every custom-check id that MUST appear in APP_END_EVIDENCE. Absent at END => FAIL. (NOTE: deposit-branch import isolation is NOT here - it is the script-enforced AS17 in scoped_deny_imports, never self-attested.) 3.1-C (P1-S4) additive extension: an item may stay a STRING (existing 'assert' mode - a CUSTOM_CHECKS.md prose 'id: PASS' line OR a passing command-evidence entry satisfies it, unchanged) OR be an OBJECT declaring mode='invariant_preserved'. For invariant_preserved a bare prose 'PASS' MUST NOT satisfy the check: it requires structured evidence - a PASSING command-evidence entry (status=pass, exit_code=0) named by test_id (else id), an additive/class-scoped diff, and the re-pin post_sha. This fixes the F1 friction (custom checks conflating 'byte-identical' with 'invariant preserved') and is the check the RT4-R1 UNLOCK_RECORD.invariant_test_id points at.

id.description: The custom-check id that must appear in evidence.

mode.description: 'assert' = legacy behaviour. 'invariant_preserved' = a bare prose PASS is insufficient; a passing command-evidence test is required.

invariant.description: Human-readable statement of the invariant the additive edit must preserve.

test_id.description: Name of the command-evidence entry (behavioral test) proving the invariant; defaults to id when omitted.

requires_additive_diff.description: Whether the change must be additive/scoped-to-class (not byte-identical).

post_sha.description: Optional recorded re-pin SHA-256 of the edited artifact.

client_money_logic_policy.description: Models AS14's client-side money-logic verification rule and the client-money invariants.

required_evidence_one_of.description: Client-side React state touching money logic needs >= 1 of these.

manual_proof_is_degraded.description: Manual browser proof is DEGRADED evidence for money-affecting state and requires explicit CLIENT_DEGRADED_ACCEPTED in HUMAN_ACCEPTANCE.md.

display_only_math_is_financial.description: Client-side display-only math is still financial logic if a user may make a decision from it.

fast_lane_policy.description: Models AS12: the RT1/RT2 fast lane is a reduced-EVIDENCE lane, never reduced-AUTHORITY. Eligibility is classifier-proven; an ineligible request FAILS (never an override).

eligible_buckets.description: Only classifier-proven cosmetic buckets are eligible; any financial/auth/backend/schema/dependency/locked/config/generated/routing/test-deletion/formatting/rounding/date/currency surface disqualifies.

still_required.description: Always still required even on the fast lane (classifier output, denylist proof, typecheck-or-smoke, full APP_END_EVIDENCE, human gate).

risk_tier_policy.description: Models the app-level tier context (the 'tier, etc.' the legacy profile influences). Per-slice DECLARED_TIER lives in the session/END evidence (AS3/E6); this records the app's tier floor and the upgrade-only principle the profile detectors feed.

real_mode_min_tier.description: Minimum tier any real-mode slice on this app is treated as (financial app => typically RT3+).

classifier_upgrade_only.description: Invariant: the classifier may only UPGRADE risk vs the derived tier (declared >= derived); declared below derived FAILS (AS3/E6).

app_memory.description: Models PROF-LOCKED-INVARIANTS and the NEO_APP.md app-memory fields the matrix homes to JSON_PROFILE (product purpose, architecture, environments, baseline, locked invariants). Read at RT2+ (RT-2). Secret VALUES never stored - key names only.

product_purpose.description: What the app does and for whom.

architecture.description: Major components, runtime topology, key data flows.

environments.description: local/sandbox/staging/production and HOW each is PROVEN (never inferred from a name - REL-6).

identity_proof.description: Marker/attestation that PROVES this environment's identity (not its label).

current_baseline.description: Last-known-good commit SHA/tag/deploy + date; updated each closeout (REL-1).

locked_invariants.description: Things that MUST NOT change without explicit approval: financial rules, schemas, public contracts, auth model, byte-exact rendering. Each cross-links its source-of-truth. PROF-LOCKED-INVARIANTS.

source_of_truth.description: Where the rule is authoritatively defined (file/path/check id).

known_risks.description: Fragile areas, past incidents, intentional residue.

test_build_commands.description: Exact install/build/typecheck/test/lint commands feeding test_plan + verifier.

rollback_notes.description: How to return to last-known-good; what is irreversible.

open_decisions.description: Pending decisions/backlog; relative dates converted to absolute.
